history.author: Jake Roach
history.titleZh: 英特尔二进制优化工具实测解析——iBOT转换如何实现高达18%、平均8%的游戏性能提升
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔在其新一代酷睿Ultra 200S Plus系列处理器中，引入了一项名为“英特尔二进制优化工具”的创新软件技术，简称iBOT。这项技术并非简单的应用优化，而是一个运行在二进制指令层面的动态翻译层。它的核心目标并非像微软Prism或苹果Rosetta那样进行跨指令集架构的转换，而是在同属x86的范畴内，将针对其他x86平台（或通用x86）编译的应用程序指令，实时“翻译”和优化为更适合英特尔特定微架构执行的指令，从而提升执行效率。简言之，它旨在将“其他x86”代码转化为“英特尔x86”代码。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

iBOT的技术根基源于英特尔的硬件性能分析能力。其工作流程主要分为两步：首先是**硬件性能分析**。在Arrow Lake Refresh及未来架构的英特尔CPU内部，集成了硬件性能计数器，能够在极低层级实时监测代码执行时发生的各种低效事件，例如缓存未命中、分支预测错误、自旋锁和硬件中断等。英特尔利用其专有的硬件辅助的性能导向优化工具链来捕获和分析这些数据。

其次是**实时指令翻译与优化**。当iBOT通过分析识别出导致性能低下的特定指令序列时，它会在应用程序运行时，在内存中动态地重写这些二进制指令，以避免已发现的低效问题。例如，如果发现某个数据因未被正确标记优先级而频繁从缓存中溢出，iBOT可以重写相关指令，为该数据打上“保持本地”的标签，从而减少缓存未命中。

**与以往技术的根本区别**：
- **动态调优技术**：关注系统级、特别是功耗的优化。
- **应用优化器**：关注应用级的线程调度，解决过度线程化等问题。
- **iBOT**：深入到编译器生成的**二进制代码**层面进行优化，无需开发者修改源代码并重新编译。这是其最大的创新点，相当于为现有软件提供了一个“后期制作”的优化阶段，无需软件开发商主动配合。

**性能表现与分析**

目前，iBOT仅在12款游戏中启用。在Tom‘s Hardware的测试中，该技术为酷睿Ultra 5 250K Plus和酷睿Ultra 7 270K Plus带来了平均约**8%的帧率提升**。

- **具体游戏表现**：提升幅度因游戏和CPU型号而异。在《古墓丽影：暗影》中，270K Plus获得了高达**18%** 的性能提升；《霍格沃茨之遗》提升超过12%。然而，在《赛博朋克2077》等游戏中，提升幅度则很小（约1.8%）。不同CPU对同一游戏的优化响应也可能不同，例如在《最终幻想14》中，250K Plus提升9%，而270K Plus仅提升5%。
- **基准测试潜力**：作为概念验证，英特尔也为Geekbench 6创建了iBOT配置文件，结果显示了**单线程性能提升约5%** ，多线程性能提升约3%。这证明了iBOT的潜力并不仅限于游戏，在单线程敏感型应用上可能带来显著收益。
- **功耗与能效**：启用iBOT后，CPU功耗和温度有极小幅上升（通常仅增加1-2瓦），同时能效有所改善，这主要是由于减少了线程空闲等待等低效情况。

**技术影响与应用场景**

1.  **提升有效IPC**：iBOT本质上是英特尔通过软件方式提升每时钟周期指令数的一个“杠杆”。它通过修补微架构层面的执行低效问题，从整体上累积出可观的性能增益。
2.  **解决软件生态滞后问题**：许多应用程序（尤其是老软件或使用旧工具链开发的软件）并未针对最新的英特尔微架构进行优化。iBOT提供了一种“向后”和“向前”兼容的优化途径，既能优化老程序在新硬件上的运行，也能帮助新程序在老硬件上更高效地运行。
3.  **降低开发者优化门槛**：开发商无需为了迎合某一特定硬件而投入资源进行深度优化和重新发布版本。英特尔可以在后台完成优化，并通过驱动程序更新将优化配置文件分发给所有用户。
4.  **平台差异化竞争力**：如果得到长期支持和广泛应用，iBOT可以成为英特尔平台一项独特的软件增值特性，增强其产品吸引力。

**挑战与展望**

尽管前景可观，iBOT仍面临挑战：
- **支持范围有限**：目前仅支持少量游戏，其广泛价值需待支持列表大幅扩展后才能充分体现。
- **优化效果不统一**：不同应用程序、甚至不同CPU型号的优化效果存在差异，未来如何管理针对庞杂硬件和应用组合的优化配置文件是一大课题。
- **长期承诺**：英特尔有推出软件优化功能但未能持续投入的历史。iBOT的成功与否，高度依赖于英特尔能否长期坚持对其进行开发和扩展。

**总结**

英特尔的iBOT是一项颇具潜力的底层二进制优化技术。它通过硬件性能监控和实时指令重写，在不修改源代码的前提下，弥合了通用x86软件与英特尔特定硬件微架构之间的效率缝隙。初期测试显示了明确的游戏性能提升，并揭示其在更广泛单线程应用中的潜力。然而，这项技术的真正影响力将取决于英特尔未来的支持力度、优化范围的扩展程度以及其应对复杂硬件软件生态的适配能力。

history.title: Save big on Intel's Arrow Lake refresh with almost $300 off on this 3-item bundle — new Core Ultra 270K Plus processor, MSI Z890 Carbon Wifi motherboard, and 32GB of G.Skill Trident Z5 DDR5-6400 RAM for $899.99
history.description: Buy a powerful Intel Core Ultra 270K Plus bundle with a MSI Z890 Carbon Wifi and 32GB of RAM for $899.99 - get pre AI-apocolypse RAM pricing on this awesome launch day Newegg bundle
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Newegg is at it again with this excellent 3-item combo, but this time, it’s offering Intel’s latest and greatest processor in the Core Ultra 270K Plus. They pair it with 32GB (2x16GB) of GSkill DDR5-6400 and a high-quality mid-range class MSI MPG Z890 Carbon motherboard for only $899.99. The $289.98 savings essentially nets the RAM at half off, and very close to pre-AI-apocalypse pricing. Check out this deal on Newegg Although Intel's sales of its Arrow Lake-based (Core Ultra) processors have been lackluster due to the popularity and performance of AMD’s Ryzen X3D processors, the new Core Ultra 270K Plus has received better reception. This is in part due to the top-notch productivity performance and the much lower price point ($319.99 at Newegg, the 285K launched at nearly $600) alone. Gaming is still an afterthought, but the new processor still performs well, just not as well as the X3D chips at lower resolutions. Save 24% ($289.98)Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus, MSI Z890 Carbon Wifi, 32GB GSkill DDR5-6400 RAM: was $1,189.97 now $899.99 at NeweggSnag Intel's new Core Ultra 9 270K Plus processor, 32GB of DDR5-6400 CL32 GSkill RAM, and a high-quality mid-range MSI MPG Z890 Carbon Wifi motherboard for $899.99. The almost $300 discount essentially places the RAM at pre AI-apocolypse pricing and is a great deal for Intel's latest and greatest processor.View Deal The new Core Ultra 270K Plus CPU boasts 24 cores/24 threads with an 8+16 (Performance and ecores) configuration. The performance cores have a base clock of 3.7 GHz and a boost of 5.4 GHz. The ecores did receive a clockspeed increase with their boost up to 4.7 GHz from 4.6 GHz. L3 Cache still sits at 76MB, as does the TDP of 125/250W. Memory support was raised from a maximum (not overclocked) of 7200 MT/s, up from 6400 MT/s on the original Arrow Lake chips. In all, it’s an improved chip, but the lower price point is where the real value is found. Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future) Next up is the MSI MPG Z890 Carbon Wifi motherboard. I reviewed this mid-range offering previously and loved the updated ‘Click X’ BIOS, DIY features, and the premium all-black aesthetic. The board supports Core Ultra Plus processors with the latest BIOS and includes a BIOS Flashback function, allowing updates without a processor, ensuring compatibility. You get five M.2 sockets (one PCIe 5.0 x4) and four SATA ports for storage, 5 GbE and Wi-Fi 7, loads of USB ports on the rear IO (13 total, 3x Type-C two of those 40 Gbps Thunderbolt 4) and some AI functionality (Overclocking, LAN, and Cooling) to help you get the most out of the system. The all-black appearance with RGBs on the heatsinks makes for an awesome centerpiece in a dark-themed build and a perfect complement to the 270K Plus in the combo.Finally, Newegg includes 32GB of GSkill Trident Z5 RGB series DDR5-6400 CL32 RAM to round out the combo. The black sticks sport a frosted RGB strip on top for some bling and match the Carbon Wifi motherboard. While the speed isn’t the fastest for the platform, you’ll still get incredible performance with this kit. And the fact that with the $289.99 discount, this RAM is actually priced like it was in mid-2025.Newegg has multiple Intel bundles out today in conjunction with the release of the Arrow Lake refresh chips, with varying levels of motherboards and discounts that are all worth a look, but this one at $899.99 has the highest discount among them. If 1080p gaming isn’t how you primarily use your PC, Intel’s updated CPUs are certainly a worthwhile purchase, especially with an almost $300 savings. If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, Gaming Chair, Best Wi-Fi Routers, Best Motherboard, or CPU Deals pages.
history.author: Joe Shields
history.titleZh: 英特尔Arrow Lake新品套装大优惠：三件套立省近300美元——全新酷睿Ultra 270K Plus处理器、微星Z890 Carbon Wifi主板与32GB芝奇Trident Z5 DDR5-6400内存套装仅售899.99美元。
history.descriptionZh:
美国电商新蛋（Newegg）近日推出了一款极具吸引力的硬件捆绑套装，将英特尔最新的酷睿 Ultra 9 270K Plus 处理器、微星 MPG Z890 Carbon Wifi 主板以及 G.Skill 的 32GB DDR5-6400 内存组合销售，售价仅为 899.99 美元，相较于单品总价 1,189.97 美元，直接节省了 289.98 美元。此次促销正值英特尔基于 Arrow Lake 架构的酷睿 Ultra 处理器（刷新版）发布之际，新蛋同步推出了多款捆绑套装，而此次推荐的组合因其折扣力度最大而尤为突出。

尽管英特尔 Arrow Lake 架构处理器的市场销售因 AMD 锐龙 X3D 系列处理器的强势表现而相对平淡，但这款新推出的酷睿 Ultra 9 270K Plus 获得了相对更好的反响。这主要归功于其卓越的生产力性能以及更具竞争力的价格（新蛋售价 319.99 美元，而初代 285K 上市价接近 600 美元）。在游戏性能方面，虽然并非其最强项，尤其是在低分辨率下仍不敌 X3D 芯片，但其表现依然可圈可点。

**核心技术规格与创新点：**
*   **处理器：酷睿 Ultra 9 270K Plus**
    *   采用 8个性能核（P-core） + 16个能效核（E-core）的设计，共24核心24线程。
    *   性能核基础频率为 3.7 GHz，最大睿频可达 5.4 GHz。能效核的睿频频率也从之前的 4.6 GHz 提升至 4.7 GHz。
    *   三级缓存维持在 76MB，热设计功耗（TDP）为 125W（基础）/ 250W（最大睿频）。
    *   关键升级点在于内存支持，其官方支持的最大内存频率（非超频）从初代 Arrow Lake 的 6400 MT/s 提升至了 7200 MT/s，为高频内存提供了更好的原生支持。

*   **主板：微星 MPG Z890 Carbon Wifi**
    *   定位中高端，采用全黑色设计，散热片上集成 RGB 灯效，适合深色主题装机。
    *   搭载了升级版的 `Click X` BIOS 界面，便于用户操作。
    *   配备 BIOS Flashback 功能，可在无处理器的情况下更新 BIOS，确保对新款处理器的即插即用兼容性。
    *   扩展性丰富：提供 5 个 M.2 插槽（其中一个为 PCIe 5.0 x4）和 4 个 SATA 接口。
    *   网络连接先进：集成了 5 GbE 有线网卡和 Wi-Fi 7 无线网卡。
    *   后置 I/O 接口充足：共 13 个 USB 接口，包括 3 个 Type-C（其中两个为 40 Gbps 的 Thunderbolt 4）。
    *   具备多项 AI 功能：如 AI 超频、AI 网络优化和 AI 散热管理，旨在帮助用户最大化系统性能。

*   **内存：G.Skill Trident Z5 RGB DDR5-6400**
    *   容量为 32GB（2x16GB），时序为 CL32。
    *   黑色马甲配以顶部的磨砂 RGB 灯条，与 Carbon 主板风格相得益彰。
    *   虽然 6400 MT/s 并非该平台支持的最高频率，但仍能提供出色的性能。更重要的是，在此捆绑优惠下，这套内存的实际价格相当于回到了 2025 年中“AI热潮”前的水平。

**性能分析与市场定位：**
该捆绑套装的核心价值在于极高的性价比。近 300 美元的折扣几乎让内存“半价”赠送，使得用户能够以接近历史低位的价格获得英特尔当前顶级的消费级平台。对于主要用途是内容创作、软件开发、多媒体制作等生产力任务的用户而言，酷睿 Ultra 9 270K Plus 强大的多线程性能结合高速 DDR5 内存和功能全面的 Z890 主板，构成了一个非常均衡且强大的组合。虽然对于追求极致帧率的 1080p 竞技游戏玩家而言，AMD 的 X3D 处理器可能是更优选择，但此套装的综合优势在预算有限且注重全能表现的用户面前显得格外突出。

**技术影响与应用场景：**
此次捆绑促销反映了英特尔在面临激烈竞争时，正采取更灵活的市场策略来提振其新一代处理器的吸引力。它将最新的计算平台以更亲民的价格推向市场，有助于加速 DDR5 内存和高性能主板的普及。该套装非常适合用于构建高性能工作站、流媒体直播主机或兼顾生产与娱乐的高端台式机。其丰富的扩展接口和高速连接能力也为未来的存储和网络升级预留了充足空间。对于希望在控制预算的同时一步到位升级到最新平台的DIY玩家和专业人士来说，这是一个颇具吸引力的选择。

history.title: Three individuals charged with attempting to break US sanctions on AI chips — damning text messages between conspirators reveal intention to find clients to ‘act as pass through partner for customers in China’
history.description: One Chinese and two U.S. citizens have been charged of conspiring to commit smuggling and export control violations after they attempted to order servers containing controlled chips for export to Thailand on paper, but with China as the intended final destination.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Three people have been indicted with conspiring to commit smuggling and export control violations after it was revealed that they intended to order over $170 million worth of computer servers containing export-controlled chips made by a California-based company. According to the Department of Justice, Stanley Yi Zheng, Matthew Kelly, and Tommy Shad English worked together in an attempt to purchase more than 1,000 servers from an American manufacturer that contained said AI chips. Zheng is a 56-year-old from Hong Kong, China, while Kelly, 49, and English, 53, are both based in the U.S. Zheng was arrested on March 22, 2026, while the two other co-conspirators surrendered to federal authorities three days later. The revelation follows a report last week where Super Micro employees were charged with smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia hardware to China.English first tried to purchase 750 servers, worth about $170 million, in October 2023 from the said server manufacturer (dubbed Company-1 in the case), of which 600 contained controlled chips. He was supposedly making the purchase on behalf of a Thailand-based company and even signed an “Advanced Computing Certification” noting that the units were not heading for China or any other country sanctioned by the United States. After transferring $20 million to Company-1 as partial payment for the order, English asked to include Zheng and Kelly in the email thread.This rang alarm bells within Company-1 as they noted that Zheng’s company is based in China and that no one from the Thailand-based company was included in the recipients. It also noted that “China is an embargoed country restricted by the U.S. government. U.S. companies are restricted from selling to businesses or end users headquartered in China.” Since Company-1 was unable to verify the supposed Thai end-user for the October 2023 order, it did not complete English’s purchase order.Article continues below You may like Supermicro employees accused of smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia hardware to China US senators want to suspend Nvidia AI chip export licenses to China and its intermediaries The Super Micro AI accelerator smuggling scandal proves how cut-throat the global AI race has become — as global trade evolves, so does export control evasion You may think that that would’ve been the end of it, but it seems that English wanted to give it another try. In April 2024, he signed up for another order — this time attempting to purchase 500 servers, again from Company-1. He signed another End User Certification saying that the servers are headed for Thailand, but, for one reason or another, the deal fell through again.Image 1 of 4(Image credit: U.S. Department of Justice)(Image credit: U.S. Department of Justice)(Image credit: U.S. Department of Justice)(Image credit: U.S. Department of Justice)The biggest evidence against the three people are messages in the group chat entitled “GPU Partnership.” These included messages with English telling Kelly, “I’m not breaking my back. I fake (sic) these weeks ago,” as well as a discussion regarding the prices of Nvidia’s A100, A800, H100, and H800 chips in China. Kelly even sent Zheng a draft solicitation message that told potential partners that “We also have a few customers in China but it’s a banned country for distribution,” and “One (sic) you can find customers that need GPUs for their supercomputer solutions or two they act as a pass through (sic) partner for customers in China.” Zheng even responded to this with “DO NOT MENTION ANYTHING ABOUT CHINA,” and “We will draw attentions (sic) from US government for embargo (sic) violation.”It’s unclear how the three were arrested by the authorities, but their activities were probably reported by Company-1, especially after English’s multiple attempts to acquire the banned chips. Furthermore, this isn’t the first case tied to AI chip smuggling that the U.S. is trying — just this month, three Supermicro employees have been indicted for smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia hardware to China. history.titleZh: 美国制裁AI芯片案三名被告获罪——短信显示其密谋"为中国客户充当中转站"
history.descriptionZh:
近日，美国司法部公布了一起涉及人工智能芯片走私的重大案件。三名嫌疑人因合谋违反出口管制法规和走私罪名被起诉，他们涉嫌企图通过虚假的中转手段，从美国一家服务器制造商（法庭文件中称为“公司-1”）采购价值超过1.7亿美元的服务器，这些服务器内含有受美国出口管制的人工智能芯片。

## 案件背景与涉案人员

本案是美国政府近期加强执行对华先进计算技术出口管制的一系列行动之一。涉案三人分别为：中国香港居民郑某某（56岁）、以及美国居民马修·凯利（49岁）和汤米·沙德·英格里希（53岁）。其中，郑某某已于2026年3月22日被捕，凯利和英格里希则于三日后向联邦当局自首。

## 走私手法与核心证据

案件的核心在于嫌疑人试图规避美国对华出口管制。2023年10月，英格里希首次尝试以一家泰国公司名义，向“公司-1”订购750台服务器（总价约1.7亿美元），其中600台包含受控芯片。他签署了《先进计算认证》，保证这些服务器不会运往中国或任何美国制裁的国家，并支付了2000万美元的部分货款。

然而，当英格里希将郑某某和凯利加入订单邮件线程后，引起了“公司-1”的警觉。该公司发现郑某某的公司位于中国，而邮件收件人中没有任何来自所述泰国公司的代表。鉴于美国禁止向总部位于中国的企业销售相关产品，“公司-1”因无法核实泰国最终用户而取消了订单。2024年4月，英格里希再次尝试订购500台服务器但同样失败。

对三人最不利的证据来自一个名为“GPU合作伙伴”的群聊记录。聊天内容显示：
*   英格里希对凯利提到：“我不是在硬撑。我几周前就（把文件）伪造好了。”
*   群内讨论了中国市场上英伟达A100、A800、H100及H800芯片的价格。
*   凯利曾向郑某某发送一份招揽潜在合作伙伴的草稿，其中写道：“我们在中国也有一些客户，但那里是禁止分销的国家”，“一是你可以寻找需要GPU用于超级计算机解决方案的客户，二是他们可以作为中国客户的中转合作伙伴”。
*   郑某某对此明确警告：“**不要提及任何关于中国的事**”，并指出“我们会因违反禁运规定而引来美国政府的注意”。

## 涉案芯片的技术背景与战略意义

此案涉及的芯片主要是英伟达的高性能AI加速器，如A100和H100系列。这些芯片因其卓越的并行计算能力和高带宽内存，已成为训练大规模人工智能模型（如大语言模型）的业界标准硬件。

*   **核心技术原理**：A100和H100基于英伟达的Ampere和Hopper架构，集成了Tensor Core核心，专门用于加速矩阵运算，这是深度学习训练和推理的核心。H800是中国市场特供的合规版本，在芯片间互联带宽上进行了限制，以符合美国的出口管制规定。
*   **性能与管制原因**：美国政府限制出口的是达到特定算力密度和互联性能阈值的芯片，以防止其被用于中国的军事现代化和高性能计算领域。直接走私原始版本的A100/H100，意在绕过这些性能限制。
*   **影响与应用场景**：获得这些尖端AI芯片，对中国的科技公司、研究机构以及可能涉及军事AI应用的单位至关重要。它们被用于数据中心、云服务、自动驾驶、科学研究和高级模拟等关键领域。因此，此类芯片成为技术竞争和出口管制的焦点。

## 行业影响与近期趋势

本案并非孤立事件。就在本月，超微电脑（Supermicro）的三名员工也因涉嫌向中国走私价值25亿美元的英伟达硬件而被起诉。这些案件揭示了在全球人工智能军备竞赛白热化的背景下，为获取关键算力硬件，非法规避出口管制的行为日益猖獗。

这些诉讼表明，美国执法部门正与芯片制造商及服务器供应商紧密合作，严查供应链中的“中转”欺诈行为。同时，案件也凸显出，随着贸易路线的演变，规避出口管制的手段也变得更加复杂，促使监管和审查机制不断升级。

这起价值1.7亿美元的服务器走私未遂案，连同近期其他类似案件，清晰地反映了先进AI芯片在全球地缘政治与技术竞争中的核心战略价值。它不仅是一次法律事件，更是当前全球高科技产业竞争与国家安全关切相互交织的缩影。

history.title: Elon Musk's Terafab semiconductor project could cost $5 trillion, Bernstein claims — herculean effort would cost more than 70% of the total yearly US government budget
history.description: To build 1 TW of AI silicon per year, Elon Musk's TeraFab would need to process 22.4 million Rubin Ultra GPU wafers, 2.716 million Vera CPU wafers, and 15.824 million HBM4E wafers annually using from 142 to 358 fabs, according to Bernstein.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 7 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Although the $20 billion funds injected in Elon Musk's TeraFab project — which is supposed to build logic and memory chips as well as package them under one roof — is barely enough to build a 7nm-class logic fab, Elon Musk's eventual ambitions include producing millions or billions of AI chips that consume 1 terawatt (1 TW) of power per year. This ambition by far exceeds today's industry capacity, and if Musk pursues it, he will need $5 trillion, according to Bernstein (via @Jukan05). Interestingly, the order of the sum is similar to what Sam Altman was seeking for his failed fab network a couple of years ago.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingTo build 1 TW of AI silicon per year, Elon Musk's TeraFab would need to process 22.4 million Rubin Ultra GPU wafers, 2.716 million Vera CPU wafers, and 15.824 million HBM4E wafers annually using from 142 to 358 fabs, according to Bernstein.The firm gets these figures, which it describes as "a very rough back-of-the-envelope wafer capacity calculation," by using a top-down approach, translating rack-level power demand into required semiconductor manufacturing capacity. Using power consumptions of rack-scale systems (120 kW for Rubin to 600 kW for Rubin Ultra), analysts convert system volumes into chip counts and then into wafer demand using their die sizes, such as ~825 mm² for GPU dies, ~800 mm² for CPU dies, the number of HBM stacks, and yields. However, Bernstein seems to overstate the typical capacity of logic fabs (50,000 wafer starts per month, WSPM, instead of 20,000 WSPM), understates the capacity of DRAM fabs (50,000 WSPM instead of 100,000 – 200,000 WSPM), and assumes prices per fab at $35 million, which likely inflates total estimates even if the multi-trillion-dollar magnitude is generally correct.Article continues below You may like Elon Musk unveils $20 billion ‘TeraFab’ chip project to make chips, memory, and package processors all under one roof Elon Musk says his Terafab Project chipmaking venture will launch in seven days Tesla hiring semiconductor fabs construction manager Trillions for fabs and packaging facilitiesBased on what we know about the modern semiconductor industry, a modern leading-edge logic fab typically delivers around 20,000 WSPM, or roughly 240,000 wafers per year. To produce 25.116 million logic wafers annually, TeraFab would require about 105 fabs at perfect yields, or 126 fabs at 80% yields. A 2nm-class capable fab costs from $25 billion to $35 billion (~$30 billion midpoint), so logic capacity alone would require around $3.15 trillion, assuming a 100% yield and $3.78 billion at 80% yield.For context, TSMC shipped 15.023 million 300-mm-equivalent wafers in 2025, which includes 200-mm wafers and 300-mm wafers made on outdated process technologies. Also important, TSMC currently operates about 50 300-mm fab modules built over two decades.Large-scale high-bandwidth memory (HBM) production is also crucially important for achieving Elon Musk's goals for TeraFab. Modern DRAM fabs — run by Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix — typically offer 100,000 to 200,000 WSPM (so, let us take 150,000 WSPM as the midpoint). Producing 15.824 million HBM4E wafers would require about 9 fabs at 100% yield, or ~12 fabs at 70% yield. Each of these fabs costs at least $20 billion, or roughly $240 billion for front-end memory capacity alone. However, HBM output is constrained by stacking and packaging capabilities and yields, not only by the output of DRAM devices. For comparison, the three major DRAM makers currently operate only ~30 fab modules built since the early 2000s.Advanced packaging facilities used for 2.5D and 3D integration, as well as HBM assembly, cost around $2 billion to $3.5 billion per phase, and TeraFab would require tens or even hundreds of such facilities to assemble AI processors and HBM stacks, which means hundreds of billions of dollars in additional investment.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAltogether, TeraFab would require well north of $4 trillion, which generally aligns with Bernstein's $5 trillion estimate, excluding land, process R&D, software, and ecosystem development.Constraints beyond moneyRaising $5 trillion would be extraordinarily difficult. For context, companies like Nvidia, Apple, and Alphabet have market capitalizations of $4.34 trillion, $3.71 trillion, and $3.5 trillion, respectively, so Musk would need to mobilize capital exceeding the value of the world's most valuable corporations. It is hard to imagine a private fundraising round, consortium, or even sovereign funding of this magnitude. For example, even if the U.S. would like to fund Musk's semiconductor venture, it could not do this that easily, as its budget for this year is about $7 trillion.The only conceivable path would involve multi-government backing, sovereign wealth funds, hyperscalers, and capital markets acting in concert. However, we doubt this is possible at all. Furthermore, at a scale of $5 trillion deployed within a foreseeable timeframe, constraints would extend beyond capital and would include limited availability of wafer fabrication equipment, construction materials, and a sufficiently large and skilled workforce to build, operate, and maintain such fabs.Then again, does Musk really plan to build a foundry that would leave behind TSMC, Samsung, and Intel combined just to make enough chips for Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI? Well, this is an open question.
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 伯恩斯坦：马斯克Terafab半导体项目成本或达5万亿美元，这笔巨额支出将占美国政府全年总预算70%以上
history.descriptionZh:
埃隆·马斯克近日宣布了名为“TeraFab”的芯片制造项目，初始投资200亿美元。该项目旨在建立一个集逻辑芯片、存储芯片制造及先进封装于一体的超级工厂。然而，其终极目标远不止于此：马斯克的雄心是每年生产消耗高达1太瓦（1 TW）电力的AI芯片。这一规模已远远超出当前全球半导体产业的整体产能。据投资机构伯恩斯坦（Bernstein）估算，若要实现这一目标，所需资金可能高达5万亿美元，这一数字甚至超过了全球市值最高的几家科技公司（如英伟达、苹果、谷歌）的市值总和，也与几年前山姆·奥特曼为其夭折的芯片工厂网络寻求的资金规模处于同一量级。

**产能需求的惊人估算**
伯恩斯坦采用了一种“粗略的纸上估算”方法，从AI集群机柜级别的功耗需求出发，逆向推算出所需的半导体制造产能。具体而言，若以特斯拉最新披露的“Rubin Ultra”GPU集群（功耗约600 kW/机柜）和“Vera”CPU等为核心计算单元，并假设使用未来的HBM4E内存，那么每年生产1 TW功耗的AI芯片将需要：
*   **逻辑芯片（GPU/CPU）：** 约2,516万片300毫米晶圆（其中GPU晶圆约2,240万片，CPU晶圆约271.6万片）。
*   **HBM内存（HBM4E）：** 约1,582万片300毫米晶圆。

**对应的工厂规模与天价投资**
基于上述晶圆需求，并结合现代半导体工厂的标准产能，可以推算出TeraFab项目所需的工厂数量及投资额：
1.  **逻辑芯片晶圆厂：** 现代尖端逻辑芯片厂（如生产2纳米级别芯片）的典型产能约为每月2万片晶圆起步（WSPM）。要满足每年约2,516万片逻辑晶圆的需求，即使在100%良率下也需要约105座这样的工厂；若以更现实的80%良率计算，则需要约126座。每座先进逻辑晶圆厂的建造成本在250亿至350亿美元之间（中位数约300亿美元）。仅逻辑芯片产能一项，投资就高达约3.15万亿至3.78万亿美元。
2.  **HBM内存晶圆厂：** 现代DRAM厂（由美光、三星、SK海力士运营）的典型产能为每月10万至20万片晶圆（中位数取15万片）。要生产约1,582万片HBM4E晶圆，在100%良率下需要约9座工厂，在70%良率下需要约12座。每座DRAM厂的成本至少为200亿美元，仅前端内存制造部分的投资就需要约2400亿美元。需要注意的是，HBM的最终产量还受限于其复杂的堆叠和封装能力与良率。
3.  **先进封装设施：** 用于2.5D/3D集成和HBM组装的高级封装设施，每期建设成本约20亿至35亿美元。TeraFab项目将需要数十甚至数百个这样的设施来组装AI处理器和HBM堆栈，这意味着还需额外投入数千亿美元。

综上所述，仅制造与封装设施的总投资就已轻松超过4万亿美元，与伯恩斯坦5万亿美元的估算基本吻合，且尚未包含土地、工艺研发、软件和生态系统开发等巨额费用。

**超越资金的根本性约束**
筹集5万亿美元资金本身已是难以想象的挑战。作为对比，美国联邦政府2025财年的总预算约为7万亿美元。这可能需要多国政府、主权财富基金、超大规模云服务商和资本市场前所未有的协同支持。
然而，挑战远不止于资金。在可预见的时间内部署如此规模的产能，将面临一系列物理和资源约束：
*   **设备与材料：** 全球半导体设备（如光刻机）和关键建筑材料的供应能力有限，无法同时满足如此多超级工厂的建设需求。
*   **人才缺口：** 缺乏足够规模且技术娴熟的劳动力来同时建造、运营和维护这上百座尖端工厂。

**项目影响与动机审视**
如果TeraFab以所述规模实现，其产能将超越台积电、三星和英特尔现有产能的总和。这将彻底重塑全球半导体产业格局。然而，一个核心问题随之而来：马斯克是否真的计划建造一个超越所有现有行业巨头的晶圆厂帝国，仅仅是为了满足其旗下特斯拉、SpaceX和xAI等公司的芯片需求？目前这仍是一个悬而未决的疑问。该项目更多地被视为一个揭示了AI芯片需求指数级增长与全球供应链极限之间巨大鸿沟的“思想实验”，其现实可行性在当前条件下极低。
